title: 5.4.3 Kruskal Algorithm
subtitle: Edge-sorting plus union-find for minimum spanning tree

content.introduction: Kruskal builds an MST by sorting edges by weight and selecting non-cycling edges using a disjoint-set structure.

sections.title: 5.4.3.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define edge-order greedy rule and cycle detection with union-find (disjoint set union).
examples: Explain why selecting smallest safe edge is valid

sections.title: 5.4.3.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe workflow: sort edges, iterate from smallest, union components when endpoints are in different sets.
examples: Process edges in sorted order and skip cycle-forming ones
Stop when MST reaches V-1 edges

sections.title: 5.4.3.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Complexity is O(E log E) dominated by sorting, with near-constant DSU operations; discuss sparse graph advantages.
examples: Compare Kruskal with Prim on sparse graphs
Estimate sorting overhead for large E
Use Kruskal in offline edge-list processing

keyTakeaways: Use union-find for efficient cycle checks
Construct MST from sorted edge lists
Analyze O(E log E) complexity behavior
Prefer Kruskal for sparse edge-centric inputs
